item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor complaint, but having researchers named Capablanca and Tal totally took me out of the article as a chess novice. It's the equivalent of having a researcher named Kalishnikov/Colt for a gun. I don't know if many will catch it, but as someone who did I don't know if I'd recommend it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the effect - the fact that it's a king piece makes one feel that it's going to be a strategizer, but no, he's an incompetent dick.

While comparatively simple to most SCPs nowadays, I think that's a good thing.

item.title: Backseat player
item.content:encoded:
I have played many wargames and I know EXACTLY the kind of jerk that this piece is lampooning. Resonates with me, personally.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. It's basically got the generic Brooklyn/Boston/D-Class voice, swears and bravado/masculinity/prejudice/misogyny/whatever and all, and that hasn't been interesting in a long, long time. It's also apparently very self-focused/not very good at the games it's in, which isn't much of a twist. The games mostly seemed selected to display to its personality, which, again, just wasn't very interesting because the personality is boring. Beyond that, the game choice didn't really do much to expand or explore the concept.

Also, the section in the containment and note describing the final incident log weren't very clear until after I had read the log, and even within the log, I'm not sure how he came into "contact" with a spreadsheet.

-1. It's a piece that knows its being played, and has an opinion about it. The base concept never really goes much beyond that: the spreadsheet example is a good lateral thought, but wouldn't be enough to carry it on its own and could use some cleanup. The personality really killed this. Inanimate objects turning out to be an angry townie is old hat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted, voice changed to East Coast American.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but you describe the piece's "voice" as British, but I can tell you from my personal experience being a Brit that no British person talks like that. Especially "ass". If you're gonna make it British, use "arse".

item.title: Re: First Skip, Here We Go
item.content:encoded:
Fixed.

The new title is yet another chess pun. Bonus points if you get it.

item.title: Re: First Skip, Here We Go
item.content:encoded: Haven't read it yet. Be aware it has the same list name as SCP-928.

item.title: First Skip, Here We Go
item.content:encoded:
Well, I done gone and done it.

Some people helped me proofread this, but I've forgotten who. If I should credit you, just tell me and I'll add you here.

Fun fact, that .pgn file is fully functional. Copy-paste it into your chess engine for some truly terrible play.
